title: Clashes Resume in Zabul and Kunar as Pakistan Vows to 'Eradicate Terrorism'
detail: Cross-border clashes resumed in Zabul province and continued in Kunar province following the expiry of the Eid ceasefire, with Afghan Taliban border forces and Pakistani military exchanging artillery and small-arms fire at multiple points along the Durand Line. Pakistan's Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar stated Pakistan remains 'firmly committed to eradicate the menace of terrorism,' signaling no change in Pakistan's strategic posture. Pakistan said its position was that the ceasefire remained intact on its side provided no attacks came from Afghan territory, a formulation Kabul dismissed. Afghan officials accused Pakistani forces of provoking the renewed exchanges. No major casualty figures were immediately released for the Zabul clashes, but local Afghan officials confirmed fighting at several border posts.

title: Pakistan FM: 'Firmly Committed to Eradicate Terrorism' — No De-escalation Signal
detail: Pakistan's Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar gave a public statement one day after the Eid ceasefire expired, declaring Pakistan 'remains firmly committed to eradicate the menace of terrorism' and that Pakistan's military actions inside Afghanistan 'are directed towards this goal.' The statement made clear Pakistan does not intend to pursue a comprehensive de-escalation and that future military operations remain on the table if TTP or ISIS-K attacks inside Pakistan continue. Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Turkey — who brokered the original Eid truce — were reported to be exploring the feasibility of a longer-term ceasefire framework, but Pakistani officials indicated no new mediation effort was imminent.
